author: @travismoore7849
text: Powdered graphite and manganese dioxide in Rochelle salt. I put two electrodes in and heated to the boiling point of water while charging the electrodes. The copper and aluminum plates built a charge in the cell. But it had low charge and you had to keep it hot to charge it up. so I called it a failure. It made a volt for a few minutes for a joule thief. So the charge separation needed to be better. It was in a 500ml container, 50 g graphite, 200g rochelle salt 25g maganese dioxide. melt salt in container  by placing it in boiling water. When melted some put in powders. Drop in electrodes and charge with a car charger based on galvanic potential. Measure charge when cooled down.

author: @daniellow426
text:
I believe I'll pretty much leave this one alone. ... Except to say this;
There is Only 'How Things Work'. We have divided that into 2 categories. [magics, miracles, etc,] and [Science]. Things we cannot explain and things we can (and can replicate).
You may have noticed as I watch your Very informative and fun videos. If you ever did then there is a Scientific reason for it, a Physics to it you might say. I wish at some point in my life people had been interested in that science enough to include me in the study of it (as an equal and sovereign individual, not an object to be studied and ignored). 
 Although I did figure out a long time ago that I needed to provide as many natural 'explanations' as I could for anything I might be accused of being able to do. After all, people do have a tendency of tying folk to stakes or strapping us into witches chairs when they don't get how we humans just haven't figured out the science of a thing yet.
 You did a masterful job of teaching us all to use a bit of critical thinking even with the simplest of videos. 
😀 Thank You.

author: @kreynolds1123
text: With quartz, The crystal itself has very very very  high resistance to the flow of electrons.  So it acts like a dielectric between two electrically conducting plates that you put on either side. When you deform the crystal, the charges are displaced inside the crystal and electrostaticly push and pull on charges in the plates and through an electrical circuit that does work.  But ideally, the plates must be aligned with how charges get distributed on a quartz crystal for the most effect. But there is no unending stream of electrons. Just a few in one direction, and then a few in the other as the crystal elastically rebounds in its resonate mode.

text: Rob I do like many of your videos, but perhaps next time you decide to delve into the field of deceptive Arts you may want to look in the mirror and realize deceptive Arts are handled much better by the people who do it for a living in example mainstream media uses deceptive art tactics everyday, the danger in doing the type of video with those stones in today's world when people click on and off there is a danger someone may have mistakenly passed on this video thinking that you found something fantastic that they could use themselves, so forgive me if I do not understand deceptive Arts tactics because I refuse to stand under them.

replies.author: @skampp
replies.text: In his defense, it literally says in the caption "this is a trick video".  I appreciate your concern about the dangers of false info, and it is a very valid frustration.  It's on account of this frustration that I hope the video is passed on... if people will watch the whole thing.  It's an eye opener that will make you think - and I believe that is the point.

replies.author: @401ksolar
replies.text: @skampp I comprehend the point, however I do not understand it, meaning I do not stand under the use of deceptive Arts, and there's good reason for that I used deceptive Arts one time in a corporate setting at a corporate board meeting, bringing up a non-issue that is easily triggering to some folks, at that meeting I did get my point across however the backlash of the board members never trusting anyone at the corporate level again led to the demise of that Corporation, so you see having a first-hand experience naively believing deceptive Arts were a good thing came with a heavy price.

author: @robertsmith4681
text: For me the sound part made it somewhat more believable, I intuitively "believed' that if a strong enough magnetic field was generated it might interfere with the microphone. how wrong was I eh ? lol

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
At least you realised that this was a bit of trickery with a bit of wizardry,

Quite unlike my hubby,,,,,because  although he's an Fantastic Musician,,,,🕶️

 He's a little bit "Vague" around the practicalities of Life, (Lmao!😆!) 

*Watching him putting a new☠️💥 lightbulb in💡,,,is Full of Terror🕯️ and Suspense!! 😬⚡✨💥😆
Entertaining,,, but Scary as Heck!!
To be honest, I must admit it almost got me because it was Robert, but I remembered an earlier video  that maybe it Almost  got  me also!!😁👍👍
Andrea and Critters.    ...XxX...
